hints.caro: 'caro' is a noun meaning 'flesh' — the subject
hints.salutare: 'salutare' is a noun (neuter) meaning 'salvation' — the direct object

hints.to seem: 'to seem' is 'videri' (passive of videre) — active 'videre' means 'to see'

hints.Present: Present would be 'videt' — 'Videbit' with -bi- is future
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'videbat' — 'Videbit' is future active

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'carnem' — 'caro' is nominative, the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'carnis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'carnis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'carnis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: 'salutare' (neuter noun) looks the same in nominative, but here it is the direct object — accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of a neuter 3rd declension noun would be 'salutari' — 'salutare' is accusative
hints.Vocative: Vocative is the same as nominative for neuters, but 'salutare' is the direct object here

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Deus' — 'Dei' with -i ending is genitive
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' is genitive (the salvation of God)

translation: All flesh shall see the salvation of God
